text.title: Postmortem Action Item SLO Tracking Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the discipline that turns the action-item tracking from folklore into a reviewable, owned, version-controlled operating tool, plus the skip path.

rule.statement: Ledger entries MUST be append-only. Corrections add new entries that reference the prior entry's id; rows are not edited in place.
rule.rationale: Editable history loses the decision trail; append-only preserves what was believed at decision time.

rule.statement: Ledger MUST use a fixed column schema: id, ts, owner, hypothesis_or_event, evidence_link, outcome, next, slo_class, slo_due. Custom per-row columns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Heterogeneous schemas destroy aggregability; a fixed schema is queryable across months.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST include `evidence_link` (commit SHA, ticket URL, dashboard URL, or transcript anchor). Entries without links are rejected at PR review.
rule.rationale: Without evidence anchors, entries are opinion, not learning.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST carry `slo_class` ∈ {P1, P2, P3} and `slo_due` = ts + class window (P1: 2 weeks, P2: 6 weeks, P3: 6 months). Items overdue MUST be flagged in the weekly digest.
rule.rationale: Action items without an SLO drift indefinitely; class + due is what makes tracking measurable.

rule.statement: Ledger MUST have a named human owner (person + role, never a team alias) and a published review cadence (weekly or monthly). Each review produces a digest + next-action list.

rationale.source: OKR review practice; Bezos weekly business review

rule.rationale: Team-aliased ownership decays; named owner + cadence is what extracts the signal.

rule.statement: Ledger entries older than the published retention horizon (default 24 months) MUST be archived to cold storage but never deleted.
rule.rationale: Long-tail learning lives in old entries; archival preserves it cheaply.

rule.statement: If team has < 3 incidents per year OR has no published action-item SLO OR has no named owner, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when review cadence cost exceeds learning return.
